experiment_record.title: Data Completion, Polynomial Fitting, and Scientific Visualization Optimization
experiment_record.description: This record documents the full workflow of CSV data completion, polynomial fitting (poly3), noise adjustment for realistic R², and generation of publication-quality heatmaps.

steps.user_request: Fill missing values in the CSV. Use column 1 as x-axis and columns 2–5 as y-axis. Ensure data follows a poly3 distribution with high R².
steps.assistant_response: Performed cubic polynomial fitting for each column independently and filled missing values while preserving known data points.

steps.user_request: Add three new rows for vol% = 10, 45, 55, ensuring consistency with poly3 fitting.
steps.assistant_response: Generated interpolated values using the fitted cubic functions and inserted them into the dataset while maintaining smooth continuity.

steps.user_request: Adjust around 15 data points so that R² is closer to 0.95 instead of ~1. Keep two decimal places and mark modified values.
steps.assistant_response: Introduced controlled perturbations (±0.05–0.15) to selected points to reduce overfitting while preserving trends. Marked modified entries with '*'.

steps.user_request: Generate a heatmap visualization: x-axis = model names, y-axis = FBn types, values = R² in percentage, color-coded by closeness to 1, with a colorbar.
steps.assistant_response: Provided Python matplotlib code for generating a publication-style heatmap with percentage labels and colorbar.

steps.user_request: Refine details: load CSV from file, use Arial font size 18, define custom red-to-brown colormap, remove ticks, and set border thickness to 3.
steps.assistant_response: Updated code to include file input, global font settings, custom colormap, removal of ticks, and thick borders for both grid and frame.

steps.user_issue: The script runs but shows no output.
steps.assistant_response: Identified that plt.close() suppressed display. Recommended adding plt.show() before closing and verifying file output path.

steps.user_request: Further refine: keep R² title, adjust colorbar to show values (100%–75%) without ticks, and fix color mapping accuracy.
steps.assistant_response: Ensured fixed vmin/vmax mapping (0.75–1.0), restored colorbar labels without tick marks, and corrected color interpolation.

steps.user_request: Improve the professionalism of the title 'R² (%)'.
steps.assistant_response: Suggested multiple academic-style titles such as 'Model Performance Comparison (R², %)' and 'Coefficient of Determination (R², %)'.

steps.user_request: Make white text bold in the heatmap.
steps.assistant_response: Added conditional fontweight='bold' for high-value cells and optional font size enhancement.

steps.user_request: For a new figure, change colorbar labels to 1.00–0.00 and update title to RMSE with professional wording.
steps.assistant_response: Provided updated tick formatting for decimal values and suggested titles such as 'Root Mean Square Error (RMSE)' and 'Comparison of Model RMSE'. Also recommended reversing colormap for RMSE interpretation.

key_methods.color_design: Linear gradient from light brown (low performance) to red (high performance)
key_methods.formatting: Arial font, size 18, thick borders, publication-quality layout

insights: Perfect polynomial fitting often leads to unrealistic R² ≈ 1, requiring noise injection.
Heatmaps for academic publications should avoid excessive ticks and emphasize clarity.
Color mapping must strictly match value ranges (vmin/vmax) to avoid misleading interpretation.
RMSE visualization requires reversed color logic compared to R².
Typography and layout (font, spacing, borders) significantly affect perceived publication quality.
